questions.question: Why is the curated observation-level dataset not public?
questions.short_answer: The full table contains restricted literature-derived extraction records and internal mapping metadata. The public release contains summary metrics, field definitions, curation rules, and a runnable synthetic example.
questions.boundary: Full extraction tables, source-derived curation materials, and row-level prediction files are handled through the manuscript data-access route.

questions.question: Can the exact manuscript metrics be regenerated from Supplementary Code 1?
questions.short_answer: No. Supplementary Code 1 demonstrates the public schema and validation paths; exact CatBoost metric recomputation uses the non-public observation table.
inspect: Supplementary Data 1 Tables S4, S6, S8, S11, and S13
questions.boundary: Toy metrics are deterministic run checks and have no membrane-performance meaning.

questions.question: How is repeated-polymer leakage handled?
questions.short_answer: The public script uses GroupKFold with polymer_group as the grouping key and reports zero overlap between train and test polymer groups in the toy example.
questions.boundary: The toy split checks the public code path; manuscript performance values come from the non-public observation-table CatBoost run.

questions.question: Why include a row-wise split if it is leakage-prone?
questions.short_answer: The row-wise split is included as an optimistic control to show why polymer-group validation is the main estimate.
questions.boundary: Row-wise numbers are not reported as deployable performance.

questions.question: Why are AEM descriptors useful when their R² increment is small?
questions.short_answer: Generic descriptors provide the main structure-derived gain over reported membrane/test covariates; AEM descriptors mainly translate fitted model behavior into follow-up measurement questions around hydration/transport balance, packing/accessibility, cation placement, and rigidity/flexibility.
inspect: Supplementary Data 1 Tables S6-S8 and S14
questions.boundary: The manuscript does not claim a large accuracy gain or causal morphology measurement from these descriptors.

questions.question: How are extrapolation risk and source-held-out offsets represented?
questions.short_answer: Support-distance and source-held-out analyses are treated as risk checks for literature-derived reported-conductivity models.
inspect: Supplementary Data 1 Tables S6, S9, and S13
questions.boundary: Post hoc source-offset check is an offset-size analysis; raw held-out predictions remain the prospective estimate.

questions.question: How should the AEM response profiles be interpreted?
questions.short_answer: The profiles are fitted-model response checks over sampled descriptor ranges and serve as prompts for follow-up membrane experiments.
questions.boundary: The public package does not include direct free-volume, morphology, hydrated-channel, or transport-pathway characterization.

questions.question: What does Supplementary Code 1 actually check?
questions.short_answer: It checks that the public schema, grouped split, leakage check, row-wise control path, feature-family loop, and claim links are runnable and inspectable without exposing restricted data.
questions.boundary: It is a public inspection example; trained models and row-level predictions follow the manuscript data-access boundary.
